architecture.summary: FRODO is a two-module framework for small-sized LMs that (1) trains an inference module to generate correct reasoning steps using an implicit causal reward function, and (2) trains a reasoning module to faithfully reason over these steps using counterfactual and causal preference objectives. The system measures and improves faithfulness by ensuring intermediate reasoning steps causally influence the final answer, addressing empirical findings that standard LLMs do not reliably use their stated reasoning when generating answers.

biblio.title: Making Reasoning Matter: Measuring and Improving Faithfulness of Chain-of-Thought Reasoning

claims.statement: LLMs do not reliably use their intermediate reasoning steps when generating an answer
FRODO significantly outperforms four competitive baselines
FRODO improves the robustness and generalization ability of the reasoning LM, yielding higher performance on out-of-distribution test sets
FRODO's rationales are more faithful to its final answer predictions than standard supervised fine-tuning
